description: Hand-curated query set for the RAG retrieval-eval harness (P17). Each query carries a free-text question plus the UC IDs that an ideal retriever should surface in the top-k. Queries are deliberately phrased in the language a Splunk admin or SOC analyst would use, not in catalog-title wording, so the eval measures semantic retrieval quality rather than tautological keyword-match. See docs/health-check-2026-progress.md §P17.

queries.text: monitor cpu utilization on linux servers
detect failed cron jobs on unix hosts
esxi host cpu contention vmware
kubernetes pod crashloopbackoff alert
aws unauthorized api call detection cloudtrail
network interface up down link flap monitoring
storage volume capacity nearing full
database slow query detection
web server http error rate alerts
detect brute force login attempts on user accounts
find dormant or stale user accounts in active directory
ransomware shadow copy deletion windows
mail flow health and queue depth monitoring
git force push to protected branch detection
splunk indexer parsing queue saturation
splunk search head cluster captain election storms
splunk hec token brute force unknown token monitoring
hvac chiller temperature anomaly building automation
ups battery health and replacement warning
itsm incident volume trend reporting
cisco ise radius authentication trend
cisco aci fabric health score telemetry
cisco ucs blade hardware fault monitoring
aws monthly cloud spend trend finops
gdpr right to erasure deletion verification
gdpr privileged access to personal data stores
pci dss log review compliance evidence
tls certificate expiration warning
dns tunneling exfiltration detection
patch compliance by team and business unit
